spec_match.rationale: Models scope_limit, max_event_states, and per-scope conversion counters. But the page treats max_event_states as a hard cap per scope when the spec actually defines it as the number of distinct attribution states per scope (used in the L1 information-bound computation), not a count of triggers — the simplification could mislead an ad-tech reader.

interactive.rationale: Add/remove scope chips, fire triggers, live budget meters with three color tiers, log feed with timestamps, reset button.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises add scopes, fire triggers, watch per-scope budgets fill, with filtered triggers flagged — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles scope_limit ceiling, exhausted budget, non-matching trigger. Misses: removing a scope while its budget has accrued (currently silently zeroed), trigger with no scope at all (legacy behaviour), trigger with multiple scopes.

references.rationale: See-also links to siblings only; the chromestatus.com/feature/5096560068395008 link is missing.

design_system.rationale: All variables, budget-bar three-tone, but uses inline onclick='removeScope(i)' which mixes HTML and JS; visually fine. The 'fire-btn' override sets background AND color together (good); reset-btn keeps the inherited dark text on light background (still readable but worth checking the global override rule applies).

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus.com/feature/<id> to references
openQuestions.detail: References section lists three sibling concepts but not the chromestatus link itself. Rule #3 — every page must carry it.

openQuestions.title: Clarify or fix the max_event_states semantics
openQuestions.detail: The spec defines max_event_states as the number of distinct attribution states per scope (entering into the information-bound noise calculation), not a count of triggers per scope. The current 'count/max' meter treats it as a usage cap which is closer to event-level rate limiting than max_event_states. Either rename the meter and document the simplification, or model the real noise/budget calculation.

openQuestions.title: Support multi-scope triggers
openQuestions.detail: The trigger picker is a single select. Real triggers can declare multiple scopes and match via set-intersection. Convert to a multi-select with a 'any-match' attribution decision.

openQuestions.title: Persist scope counters on remove
openQuestions.detail: When a scope is removed mid-experiment its accrued budget vanishes. Probably the more honest behaviour is to keep the historical row in the log section even after the live chip is gone, so the visitor can see the full session history.

summary: Most-interactive of the four attribution-scopes concepts but technically the loosest. The max_event_states meter conflates two spec concepts and the chromestatus link is missing. Fix both before this is a polished demo.
